idea_summary.topic: 30-second student demos low-cost water filter prototype

rankings.why: TikTok's short-form format is ideal for a 30-second prototype demo. Stars of Science posts on TikTok show the highest engagement rates for product reveal content among Arab youth.

score_breakdown.label: Semantic match
score_breakdown.reason: Matched Stars of Science TikTok posts share strong overlap with water-tech and product-demo framing.

score_breakdown.label: Format history
score_breakdown.reason: TikTok historically suits product demo content in the Stars of Science format model.

score_breakdown.label: Performance strength
score_breakdown.reason: Top matched TikTok posts show strong relative reach and engagement in the dataset.

score_breakdown.label: Goal alignment
score_breakdown.reason: TikTok aligns well with the applications goal based on Stars of Science usage patterns.

score_breakdown.label: Language fit
score_breakdown.reason: Mixed-language content performs well on TikTok in the Arab world.

score_breakdown.label: Duration fit
score_breakdown.reason: 30-second clips match TikTok's optimal content window for this audience.

supporting_patterns: Short-form prototype reveals drive high saves and shares on TikTok
Arabic-language captions increase reach among Gulf and Levant audiences
Hook-first editing style aligns with Stars of Science TikTok top performers

top_evidence.claim: Stars of Science TikTok posts featuring working prototypes averaged 2.1× the platform median reach
Water and environment innovation posts on TikTok show strong re-share behaviour in MENA

rankings.why: Instagram Reels and carousel posts give strong visual reach for prototype demos. Stars of Science performs consistently well here with photo + short-video hybrid content.

score_breakdown.label: Semantic match
score_breakdown.reason: Matched Stars of Science Instagram posts share overlapping themes with product demo ideas.

score_breakdown.label: Format history
score_breakdown.reason: Instagram suits product demo and announcement content in the Stars of Science format model.

score_breakdown.label: Performance strength
score_breakdown.reason: Matched Instagram posts show good relative engagement in the dataset.

score_breakdown.label: Goal alignment
score_breakdown.reason: Instagram aligns well with the applications goal for Stars of Science campaigns.

score_breakdown.label: Language fit
score_breakdown.reason: Mixed-language captions work well on Instagram for Arab-world audiences.

score_breakdown.label: Duration fit
score_breakdown.reason: 30-second Reels sit within Instagram's recommended content window.

supporting_patterns: Carousel + Reel hybrid posts outperform single-image posts for prototype reveals
Stars of Science Instagram posts with clear CTA see higher story saves
Visual proof moments (before/after) drive comment engagement on Instagram

top_evidence.claim: Stars of Science Instagram Reels featuring student inventors averaged 1.7× the account baseline reach

rankings.why: YouTube suits longer-form follow-up content. A 30-second clip works as a teaser but the platform rewards depth — consider a 3-minute explainer as the main piece.

score_breakdown.label: Semantic match
score_breakdown.reason: Stars of Science YouTube posts overlap in topic but favour longer educational formats.

score_breakdown.label: Format history
score_breakdown.reason: YouTube suits educational and explainer content more than quick demos.

score_breakdown.label: Performance strength
score_breakdown.reason: Matched YouTube posts show strong watch-time signals in the Stars of Science dataset.

score_breakdown.label: Goal alignment
score_breakdown.reason: YouTube serves the applications goal well through in-depth storytelling.

score_breakdown.label: Language fit
score_breakdown.reason: Bilingual content with subtitles performs well on YouTube MENA.

score_breakdown.label: Duration fit
score_breakdown.reason: 30-second content underperforms on YouTube where 3-10 minute content is optimal.

supporting_patterns: Stars of Science YouTube posts with full project walkthroughs average 3× more watch time
Short teasers work as YouTube Shorts to drive subscribers to the main channel
Subtitles in Arabic and English double average view duration

top_evidence.claim: Stars of Science YouTube videos over 3 minutes received 4× more comments than clips under 1 minute

rankings.why: LinkedIn reaches sponsors and industry decision-makers but engagement for short video is lower than on consumer platforms. Best used as a secondary channel for the applications goal.

score_breakdown.label: Semantic match
score_breakdown.reason: Stars of Science LinkedIn posts focus on professional milestones more than live demos.

score_breakdown.label: Format history
score_breakdown.reason: LinkedIn suits announcement and achievement content rather than product demos.

score_breakdown.label: Performance strength
score_breakdown.reason: LinkedIn posts in the dataset show moderate engagement relative to other platforms.

score_breakdown.label: Goal alignment
score_breakdown.reason: LinkedIn has some alignment with applications goal through professional network reach.

score_breakdown.label: Language fit
score_breakdown.reason: English-first content outperforms on LinkedIn in the MENA professional audience.

score_breakdown.label: Duration fit
score_breakdown.reason: Short-form video is less native to LinkedIn but performs acceptably as a teaser.

supporting_patterns: Stars of Science LinkedIn posts perform best when framing innovation as a professional achievement
Posts with tagged universities and research institutions see 2× connection growth
Text-heavy posts with embedded video outperform video-only posts on LinkedIn

top_evidence.claim: Stars of Science LinkedIn posts mentioning finalist names received 1.4× more impressions than generic posts

rankings.why: X has limited Stars of Science post volume and lower organic reach for video content. Works for real-time updates during broadcast but not ideal for reaching new applicants.

score_breakdown.label: Semantic match
score_breakdown.reason: Stars of Science X posts are sparse and mostly text-based announcements.

score_breakdown.label: Format history
score_breakdown.reason: X suits real-time announcements and news but not sustained product demo campaigns.

score_breakdown.label: Performance strength
score_breakdown.reason: Matched X posts show below-average engagement in the Stars of Science dataset.

score_breakdown.label: Goal alignment
score_breakdown.reason: X can support the applications goal for real-time season announcements.

score_breakdown.label: Language fit
score_breakdown.reason: Arabic-language posts perform modestly on X in the Gulf region.

score_breakdown.label: Duration fit
score_breakdown.reason: Short video clips are native to X and perform well when paired with a strong tweet.

supporting_patterns: X works best for Stars of Science as a live-broadcast companion channel
Thread-style posts with one clip per tweet outperform single-video posts
Hashtag campaigns tied to season launch dates drive the highest X impressions

top_evidence.claim: Stars of Science X posts during season launch windows saw 3× normal impression volume

methodology_note: The AI Reviewer ranks only the five official Stars of Science platforms. Each score blends semantic similarity to matched Stars of Science posts, historical format fit, relative post performance inside the local dataset, goal alignment, and language fit. The score is directional and evidence-backed; it is not a forecast of guaranteed views.
